title: Confusing button label after editing 2FA authenticator
author: brookmiles
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. Edit the Authenticator app under Two-factor Auth settings 2. Complete the registration by entering the 2FA code 3. Observe the button displayed below the recovery codes. ### Expected behaviour A button label of "Done" or similar. ### Actual behaviour A button that reads "Resume application authorization". ### Detailed description The word "Resume" implies that some process had been stopped or interrupted and this button will re-active or continue that process, but as far as I can tell, the process is complete at this point, no further action is required by the user, and there is nothing to resume. It's also not entirely clear what "application authorization" means... I assume this is is awkward phrasing of something like "authenticator registered", but the status message at the top of the screen already says this. A button that simply says "Done" or "OK" would make more sense in this situation. ### Mastodon instance sunny.garden ### Mastodon version v4.6.3 ### Browser name and version Firefox 152.0.5 ### Operating system LMDE 7 ### Technical details _No response_

title: Advanced Interface: Pagination for Announcements Pevents Interaction With Emoji Picker
author: belling
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem Repro Steps: 1. Use Advanced Web Interface 2. Have more than one active announcement to trigger pagination 3. Open announcement and attempt to choose an emoji ### Expected behaviour Emoji picker icon is clickable ### Actual behaviour Emoji picker icon is not clickable ### Detailed description Mousing over pagination navigation shows how it is taken out of flow by absolute positioning and inheriting a z-index that allows it to overlap emoji picker: Changing z-index on pagination to 0 makes emoji picker clickable but pagination becomes unclickable. Changing padding on pagination resolves issue. From padding:15px; to padding:15px 8px; ### Mastodon instance https://sunny.garden/deck/ ### Mastodon version v4.6.3 ### Browser name and version Confirmed in FF latest and Safari latest, not tested in others ### Operating system macOS 15.7.7, also confirmed by other user in FF latest on Linux (flavor not specified) ### Technical details _No response_

title: Some Additional Tips
author: Quasi-Arte
body:
### Pitch

Mastodon is a nice social network, but it needs a lot of improvement because, in its current state, I'm not satisfied with its functionality and the cleanliness and/or orderliness of its layout and design.

- I proposed pre-screening and moderating incoming messages before users decide to publish them.

- An increase in the character limit to at least 800-1,000.

- A better system for cataloging images and videos into albums (and/or categories/folders).

- A system (similar to Facebook) for writing short messages, aphorisms, jokes, and thoughts.

- The ability to see how many people visit my profile and read my posts.

- Ability to create at least 5-10 profiles (or 4-9 sub-profiles managed by the main profile) with the same email address (as is possible on Facebook by creating multiple public pages).

- Ability to scroll through all the images uploaded to any profile in slideshow mode (using the side arrows on your computer keyboard or horizontal scrolling on a smartphone). This feature is extremely convenient because it would allow you to merge all posts into one continuous stream without having to open the images individually for each post you scroll through.

### Motivation

-----

title: Message Moderation
author: Quasi-Arte
body:
### Pitch

I'm using Mastodon but I just can't find the feature to moderate messages before I approve them for publication... I can't find this feature... I wonder if it exists... Thanks.

### Motivation

--------------

title: Moderation: Collections are not removed by Suspend & Delete data
author: CyberTailor
body:

1. Create a dummy user
2. Sign in as the dummy user, create a collection
3. Sign in as the admin, suspend the dummy user, delete data immediately
4. Unsuspend and enable the dummy user
...

The dummy user has no collections

### Actual behaviour

All collections of the dummy user are kept intact

If this is happening on your own Mastodon server, please fill out those:

title: `og:image` parsing ignores presence of multiple images
author: clarfonthey
body:
### Steps to reproduce the problem

Example website whose image previews fail to parse: <https://txt.ltdk.xyz>

<details><summary>Relevant meta tags</summary>

```
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/lw.svg property=og:image>
<meta content=text/svg+xml property=og:image:type>
<meta content=1160 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=610 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/lw.png property=og:image>
<meta content=image/png property=og:image:type>
<meta content=1160 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=610 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/sw.svg property=og:image>
<meta content=text/svg+xml property=og:image:type>
<meta content=400 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=210 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/sw.png property=og:image>
<meta content=image/png property=og:image:type>
<meta content=400 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=210 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/l.svg property=og:image>
<meta content=text/svg+xml property=og:image:type>
<meta content=600 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=600 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/l.png property=og:image>
<meta content=image/png property=og:image:type>
<meta content=600 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=600 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/s.svg property=og:image>
<meta content=text/svg+xml property=og:image:type>
<meta content=200 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=200 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
<meta content=https://ht.ltdk.xyz/thumb/face/dk/s.png property=og:image>
<meta content=image/png property=og:image:type>
<meta content=200 property=og:image:width>
<meta content=200 property=og:image:height>
<meta content="light-dark face" property=og:image:alt>
```

</details>

### Expected behaviour

The link preview should correctly parse the image

### Actual behaviour

It does not

### Detailed description

Per the OpenGraph spec, multiple `og:image`s can be provided for a page, e.g. in multiple formats or aspect ratios: https://ogp.me/#array

However, the backend completely ignores this and just picks the first occurrence of each attribute listed: https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/blob/ea3d8a82c244cccb3c3a7673c206e3668fd3a8d3/app/lib/link_details_extractor.rb#L247-L249



### Mastodon instance

toot.cat

### Mastodon version

v4.7.0-alpha.1+glitch.bbe0c4c7a

### Technical details

In order to correctly follow the spec, the code should keep in mind where `og:image` tags occur and choose an image based upon the first `og:image` it finds which is valid. Ideally, it should also be able to choose the image with the closest aspect ratio, but at least it should be able to skip formats it doesn't support.

For example, the first (and preferred) formats included on my page are SVG, but Mastodon immediately fails in these cases because it does not support this, and then does not check for the following images, which are PNG.

title: Animate the height change when expanding and hiding CW’d or filtered posts
author: daprice
body:
### Pitch

When the height of a post in the timeline changes because I expand or hide a CW, it should animate the change in height smoothly as long as Reduce Motion isn’t turned on. If Reduce Motion is turned on, it might be better not to animate the height change but the newly shown content should nicely fade in or something.

### Motivation

Having everything just suddenly be in different positions on the screen without being able to see how they moved or what was added/taken away is really jarring.

title: Expanding a content warning within a quote post also expands any occurrences of the same post outside of the quote
author: daprice
body:
### Steps to reproduce the problem

1. Find a quote post in my timeline where the post being quoted has a CW, and the same post also exists in my timeline outside of the quote
2. Expand the CW within the quote post

### Expected behaviour

Only the CW I tapped should expand

### Actual behaviour

All occurrences of the same post have their CWs expanded. If the post is at least moderately long, this causes the one I tried to expand to be scrolled offscreen. Hiding it causes both occurrences of the post to be scrolled offscreen.

### Detailed description

In this screen recording, I’m only tapping the bottommost CW to expand or hide it, but the other CW’d post (which is the same post as the one being quoted) also expands and hides which throws off the scroll position wildly

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/25d2a176-95c4-40ed-8d31-539dab3676de

### Mastodon instance

mastodon.online

### Mastodon version

v4.7.0-nightly.2026-07-09

### Browser name and version

Safari

### Operating system

iPadOS 27 beta 3

### Technical details

_No response_

title: Repeatedly opening the emoji picker causes strange highlighting
author: daprice
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. Tap the emoji button to open the emoji picker 2. Tap outside of the emoji picker to close it 3. Repeat steps 1-2 ### Expected behaviour The emoji picker should open and close, and nothing else should happen ### Actual behaviour Random things on the screen become highlighted ### Detailed description After multiple attempts to reproduce this, it seems like a different thing becomes highlighted on subsequent attempts. In this screenshot, it seems to be a hidden overlay that’s highlighted, but I’ve also had it highlight the character limit in the composer, the “Search” placeholder in the search field, and seemingly random other things. ### Mastodon instance mastodon.online ### Mastodon version v4.7.0-nightly.2026-07-09 ### Browser name and version Safari ### Operating system iPadOS 27 beta 3 ### Technical details This might be an iPadOS 27 beta Safari bug because I can’t reproduce it on my iPhone or Mac that aren’t running the beta.

title: Emoji picker opens partially offscreen
author: daprice
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. Tap the emoji button to open the emoji picker ### Expected behaviour It should be on screen so you can use it ### Actual behaviour It opens at the bottom of the screen, then after about a quarter of a second it suddenly moves to the top of the screen, but the top bar (where the emoji categories are) is off screen ### Detailed description ### Mastodon instance mastodon.online ### Mastodon version v4.7.0-nightly.2026-07-09 ### Browser name and version Safari ### Operating system iPadOS 27 beta 3 ### Technical details _No response_

title: Emoji picker shows blurry versions of Twemoji when Auto or Native emoji is selected
author: daprice
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. In Mastodon preferences, set emoji display to Auto or Native 2. Go back to the main interface and open the emoji picker. Scroll past the instance’s custom emoji to the standard ones. ### Expected behaviour The emoji picker should show native emoji ### Actual behaviour The emoji picker shows blurred versions of Twemoji in the picker (instance custom emoji aren’t blurred, only the Twemoji are), but native emoji in the actual post composer and content. ### Detailed description ### Mastodon instance mastodon.online ### Mastodon version v4.7.0-nightly.2026-07-09 ### Browser name and version Safari ### Operating system iPadOS 27 beta 3 ### Technical details This doesn’t occur on iOS 26.x.x, so I suspect your emoji picker library is doing some user agent sniffing and doesn’t like whatever the beta Safari is returning, or this is some kind of fallback when the browser’s Unicode version is newer than what it supports.

title: Change subscribed languages modal shows no languages
author: ignisf
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. Visit any account 2. ... -> Change subscribed languages ### Expected behaviour See the last languages the account posted in ### Actual behaviour Empty list (see screenshot) ### Detailed description I am no longer able to select which languages should be subscribed for an account, see screenshot: ### Mastodon instance social.petko.me ### Mastodon version v4.6.3 ### Browser name and version Firefox 152.0.3 ### Operating system opensuse ### Technical details I believe https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/pull/37626 broke it with appending a view to the timeline key here (CC-ing @ChaosExAnima): https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/blob/5e1d0f339bfb6f74e71a84c1d7a33f6818187c0a/app/javascript/mastodon/actions/timelines_typed.ts#L93-L101 Queried here: https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/blob/5e1d0f339bfb6f74e71a84c1d7a33f6818187c0a/app/javascript/mastodon/features/subscribed_languages_modal/index.jsx#L23-L27

title: Implementations of fep-ef61
author: LinkCodeDev
body:
### Pitch

Will Mastodon ever support the [Fediverse Enhancement Proposal ef61](https://codeberg.org/fediverse/fep/src/branch/main/fep/ef61/fep-ef61.md), which introduces complete portability of accounts and ActivityPub objects? After a quick survey, I'm almost certain it's one of the most requested features, especially by those who use BlueSky rather than the Fediverse.
This would firstly allow Mastodon to function as a single decentralized social network rather than a collection of separate instances, and secondly, if I understand correctly, it would allow users to publish, for example, on Mastodon and PeerTube with a single account (once PeerTube also implements it), thus avoiding the need for multiple accounts to publish videos, images, etc.

### Motivation

The idea to open this issue came from a discussion with the developers of Cartes.app, who chose to use Bluesky rather than Mastodon because Mastodon accounts are not portable and cannot be switched instances. Bluesky doesn't want to implement ActivityPub because the current instances don't support account portability.
Many people (including myself) hesitate to create an account because they have to choose an instance. While portability already exists, it doesn't allow logging into multiple instances with a single account.
Now that account and object portability has a specification, I think it's a shame not to implement it.

title: Provide pre-built client assets
author: progval
body:
### Pitch

There are currently two main ways to install Mastodon:

1. from source (Git repository or release tarball), following the [official documentation](https://docs.joinmastodon.org/admin/install/)
2. from the undocumented Docker image, which ships with [pre-built assets](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/blob/5360faa8994b18a1d60d47866ac4ff40b3ff3bc2/Dockerfile#L369)

Could you add an alternative to option 1 where users can download a tarball from the [release tab](https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/releases/) that also contains pre-built assets for the given release?

They could be built by the GitHub CI, for example.

### Motivation

Building assets requires a significant amount of RAM (2 to 4GB), more than to run a small instance.

This means that future instance administrations who want to run Mastodon on a small VM are forced to go through the undocumented process of installing with Docker, as they cannot build assets themselves.

title: Recent mastodon versions return 401 when receiving a message from Hubzilla
author: git-marijus

title: Adjust Limit for Hashtag-Timeline Querys
author: HurricaneWeasel
body:
### Pitch

Fetching hashtag timelines is limited to [4 hashtags](https://docs.joinmastodon.org/methods/timelines/?utm_source=chatgpt.com#query-parameters-1). Better infrastructure should allow a higher number of hashtags per query. 

Please either adjust the number of hashtags, taking technical developments into account, or make this a setting in the Admin Panel to let the admin decide how many hashtags can be fetched per query.

### Motivation

Users need to set up multiple timelines to circumvent this limitation. That is bothersome.

title: "View all pinned posts" button on profile pages has an incorrect width
author: nileane
body: ### Steps to reproduce the problem 1. Navigate to a profile page containing at least two pinned posts 2. Scroll down to view the "View all pinned posts" button ### Expected behaviour "View all pinned posts" button should be full-width ### Actual behaviour "View all pinned posts" button is too short horizontally ### Detailed description Looking at the CSS from the browser inspector, changing: ``` ._feat_account_timeline__pinnedViewAllButton { ... width: calc(100% - 48px); } ``` to: ``` ._feat_account_timeline__pinnedViewAllButton { ... width: calc(100% - 32px); } ``` resolves the issue. ### Mastodon instance nileane.fr, mastodon.social ### Mastodon version v4.6.3 ### Browser name and version Chromium 150.0.7871.47 ### Operating system macOS 26.5 (stable), iOS 27.0 (Beta 2) ### Technical details _No response_

title: Customize the email template for report notifications
author: offbyone
body:
### Pitch

Much like Mastodon supports templating the webhook notification format in `/admin/webhooks/:id`, it would be great to be able to customize the format of the email sent to admins when a report is received. Currently it's in the i18n string `admin_mailer.new_report.body`, with fixed parameters.

### Motivation

As an admin, I want more information in those emails to save me clicks into the interface to deal with them. At _least_ I want the comment! I can get this in discord notifications, but having it by email would be incredibly helpful.

title: Add link verification for profile links to own posts or collections
author: baknu
body:
### Pitch

A user might want to add a link to one of their own posts or collections in their profile. These kind of links shoud be verified automatically (by including a rel="me" link somewhere (in)visisble on these pages).

### Motivation

Currently these links to own posts or collections are not verified, making them look less credible.
